varieties.name: Sambucus australasica (Yellow Elderberry) 🌏 INDIGENOUS
varieties.origin: Endemic to eastern Australia (Queensland, New South Wales). Native to Brisbane region coastal districts and rainforest margins.
varieties.traits: Shrub or small tree 2-5m tall. Pinnate leaves with 3-5 narrow elliptic leaflets, coarsely toothed. White flowers in large flat-topped clusters 10-20cm diameter.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good yields in suitable conditions. Berries ripen October-March.
varieties.availability: Available from Brisbane native plant nurseries, Queensland bushfood specialists. Lamington Native Nursery, local bushfood growers.

varieties.name: Black European Elderberry
varieties.origin: Europe, naturalized worldwide. Traditional hedgerow plant, centuries of cultivation.
varieties.traits: Deciduous shrub/small tree 3-6m tall. Dark green pinnate leaves. Large flat-topped clusters of creamy-white fragrant flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer when established. Produces on 1-year-old wood.
varieties.availability: Widely available Brisbane nurseries: Ross Creek Tropicals, Daley's Fruit Trees, Mr Fothergill's, Garden Express, Food Forest Seeds Australia.

varieties.name: Black Beauty
varieties.origin: UK, Horticulture Research International East Malling, 2001. Patent PP12,305.
varieties.traits: Upright dense shrub 2.5-3m tall and wide. Dark purple-red foliage, purple stems. Ornamental variety. Lemon-scented pink flowers in large clusters.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields when paired with pollinator (Black Lace, Laced Up, or Instant Karma).

varieties.name: Black Lace
varieties.origin: UK breeding program, released as 'Eva' PP15,575. East Malling Research.
varieties.traits: Upright shrub 1.8-2.4m tall and wide. Deeply dissected purple-black lacy foliage (similar to Japanese maple). Compact growth habit.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields; primarily grown for ornamental value.

varieties.name: Instant Karma
varieties.origin: Netherlands breeding, S. nigra 'Sanivalk'. Recent introduction.
varieties.traits: Variegated elderberry. Blue-green leaves with creamy-white margins that start gold in spring. Upright habit.
varieties.availability: Ornamental nurseries, Proven Winners retailers in Australia.

varieties.name: Laced Up
varieties.origin: Recent breeding program. Compact fastigiate selection.
varieties.traits: Unique upright columnar habit 3m tall x 1.5m wide. Lacy near-black foliage. Space-saving vertical form.

varieties.name: Adams (Adams #1 & #2) 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Selected from wild in New York, USA, 1926. One of oldest cultivated varieties. Historical heirloom selection.
varieties.traits: Vigorous upright shrub 1.8-3.6m tall. Glossy green foliage. Large white flower clusters. Adams #2 has largest fruit of all elderberries.
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely hardy and productive. Early ripening (late August-early September).

varieties.name: York 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: New York Agricultural Experiment Station, 1964. Hybrid: Ezyoff × Adams #2.
varieties.traits: Large upright shrub 1.8-4.5m tall, 1.8-2.4m wide. Vigorous, somewhat spreading habit.
varieties.yield_notes: HIGHEST YIELDING variety - 2-5kg per plant. Ripens mid-late August.
varieties.availability: International suppliers, specialized Australian fruit nurseries.

varieties.name: Johns 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: Kentville Research Station, Nova Scotia, Canada, 1954. Open-pollinated seedling of Adams.
varieties.traits: Vigorous upright tall shrub 3-3.6m tall and wide. Medium-sized white flower clusters. 3m canes.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields. Early producer. Pick berries by hundreds in clusters.

varieties.name: Nova 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: AgCanada Research Station, Kentville, Nova Scotia, 1959. Open-pollinated seedling of Adams #2.
varieties.traits: Multi-stemmed upright shrub 1.8m tall and wide. Wide clusters of creamy-white flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields. EARLIEST ripening cultivar (early August). Excellent for wine, juice, jam, jelly.

varieties.name: Bob Gordon
varieties.origin: Discovered Osceola, Missouri, USA, 1999. University of Missouri selection.
varieties.traits: Vigorous shrub. Unique inverted/down-hanging berry heads (reduces bird damage). Strong stems.
varieties.yield_notes: EXTREMELY PROLIFIC - up to 3× yield of other varieties (close to triple!). 4× antioxidant value of cherries.

varieties.name: Wyldewood
varieties.origin: Selected from wild near Eufaula, Oklahoma, USA, 1995. University of Missouri release, 2010.
varieties.traits: Enthusiastic producer. Most stems bear 3 umbels. Slow to establish, later ripening.
varieties.yield_notes: Potential 12,000 lbs per acre! First variety to bud break, ripens later in season.

varieties.name: Ranch
varieties.origin: Found at abandoned homestead, planted late 1800s. University of Missouri release, 2011. TRUE HEIRLOOM HISTORY.
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf variety, only 1.5m tall. Vigorous, heavy yielding. Strong upright stems. Single-stem fruit production. Stocky shape, lower fruiting canopy.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields despite small size. EARLIEST determinate variety. Quickest to root from cuttings.

varieties.name: Pocahontas
varieties.origin: Selected Pocahontas, Arkansas, USA. University of Missouri release, 2019.
varieties.traits: Fast growing, robust growth, substantial yield. Impressively large flower cymes up to 60cm across! Late blooming.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer - among highest yielding varieties. Determinate.

varieties.name: Blue Elderberry (Sambucus nigra ssp. cerulea)
varieties.origin: Western North America (British Columbia to Mexico). Native range includes hot inland areas.
varieties.traits: Large shrub/small tree 1.8-9m tall (can reach 30ft!). Yellow-white flowers in flat-topped clusters. Blue-waxy bloom on berries.
varieties.availability: Western US nurseries, native plant specialists.

varieties.name: Mexican Elderberry (Sambucus mexicana/nigra ssp. caerulea)
varieties.origin: Southwestern USA, Mexico. Canyons and valleys, elevations to 5,000ft, Sonoran and Chihuahuan deserts.
varieties.traits: Large shrub/small tree 6-9m tall, 6m spread. Fast growing. Multiple trunks, arching outward. Glossy green leaves, 3-9 leaflets. Unique butter-yellow flowers.
varieties.availability: Southwestern US native plant nurseries, seed specialists.

varieties.name: Sambucus javanica (Chinese Elder) 🌏 INDIGENOUS 🏆 TRADITIONAL
varieties.origin: Native to subtropical and tropical Asia: Bhutan, Burma, Cambodia, China (south), INDIA, Indonesia, Japan, Laos, Malaysia, Philippines, southern Thailand, Vietnam.
varieties.traits: Perennial herb or small shrub 1-2m tall (3-6.5ft). Pinnate leaves with 5-9 opposite leaflets. Terminal inflorescence with yellow nectaries. White to cream-colored flower clusters.
varieties.availability: Local Kerala herbalists, traditional medicine suppliers, Indian native plant nurseries. May be wild-harvested in some regions.

varieties.name: Black European Elderberry
varieties.origin: Europe, widely naturalized. Traditional variety.
varieties.traits: Shrub/small tree 3-6m. Dark green pinnate leaves. Large creamy-white flower clusters.
varieties.growing_notes: Not traditionally grown in lowland Kerala. Better suited to cooler regions like Munnar, Wayanad (hill stations). Used mainly for medicinal purposes in Ayurvedic medicines where cultivated.
varieties.availability: Limited availability in Kerala. May be sourced from hill station nurseries, specialized herb gardens, international suppliers.

varieties.name: American Black Elderberry (wild type)
varieties.origin: Native to eastern North America, from Canada to Gulf Coast.
varieties.traits: Multi-stemmed shrub 1.8-3.6m tall. Adaptable to various soil types including wet soils.
varieties.availability: Limited in Kerala. May be available through international seed suppliers, specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Adams 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: New York, USA, 1926. Historic selection.
varieties.traits: Vigorous upright shrub. Large white flower clusters.

varieties.name: York 🏆 HEIRLOOM
varieties.origin: New York Agricultural Experiment Station, 1964.
varieties.traits: Large upright shrub, vigorous.

varieties.name: Ranch
varieties.origin: Late 1800s homestead plant. University of Missouri, 2011.
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf 1.5m tall. Strong upright stems.

varieties.name: Pocahontas
varieties.origin: Pocahontas, Arkansas. University of Missouri, 2019.
varieties.traits: Fast growing, robust. Extremely large flower cymes (60cm). Late blooming.

varieties.name: Blue Elderberry (Sambucus cerulea)
varieties.origin: Western North America, including hot desert regions.
varieties.traits: Large shrub/tree 1.8-9m. Yellow-white flowers.
varieties.availability: Western US native plant nurseries, seed specialists.

varieties.name: Mexican Elderberry (Sambucus mexicana)
varieties.origin: Southwestern USA, Mexico. Desert and hot canyon regions.
varieties.traits: Large shrub/tree 6-9m. Fast growing. Unique butter-yellow flowers.
